project_acceptance.observable: Exact access_token keys are redacted case-insensitively at every nesting depth through both shared and CLI entry points.
access_token_hint, token_type, and existing recursive behavior are preserved.

tasks.objective: Verify the CLI adapter inherits the shared behavior without adding policy.

context_refs.claim: The adapter delegates to sanitize before serialization and should remain policy-free.

acceptance.observable: Focused tests cover sanitize and format_event with nested access tokens.

tasks.objective: Add exact case-insensitive access_token matching to the shared sanitizer secret-key policy.

context_refs.claim: The shared sanitizer is the single policy boundary for API and CLI callers.
SECRET_KEYS already provides exact case-insensitive key matching while recursion handles mappings and lists.

acceptance.observable: access_token and ACCESS_TOKEN values become [redacted] without prefix over-match.

tasks.handoff: Hand the shared behavior to adapter verification.

assumptions.statement: Extending SECRET_KEYS is sufficient because the existing sanitizer already recurses and compares casefolded complete keys.
